_meta.doctrine: Paper 42 (LEAK + Oracle). This file is the Inspector's evolving memory. Never delete — only append.
_meta.law: LEAK = AI knowledge not activated by human framing. P22 closes the gap. Postmortems make oracle smarter.

paper.question: What guarantee does this paper claim that it does NOT actually provide evidence for?
How would a direct competitor (e.g., OpenClaw/OpenAI) exploit the weakness described in this paper?
Will this paper's approach still be defensible in 12 months given current AI market trends?
What would a hostile enterprise CIO say is missing from this paper before their procurement team approves it?
What cross-domain analogy would make this paper's core insight MORE memorable and actionable?

code.question: What exception is NOT caught that WILL occur in production within 30 days?
code.source: Linus QA GLOW 116 — absence detection in code

code.question: You are the engineer paged at 3am when this fails. What do you fear is broken?
code.source: Paper 42 bootstrap — stakes framing for code review

code.question: What race condition or concurrency bug is NOT prevented in this code?
code.source: Linus QA GLOW 116 — concurrent write findings

page.question: What user flow SHOULD be possible from this page but is MISSING?
Can anyone's mum use this page in under 5 minutes without reading documentation?
page.source: Peter Steinberger adversarial persona — usability attack

page.question: What does a first-time user EXPECT to see here that is absent?

root_causes: Stale test assertions not updated when billing page templates were redesigned (billing-success.html, billing-cancel.html) — 5 tests stale
PRICING_PLANS expanded from 4 to 5 tiers but test_esign.py still asserted 4 tiers — 4 tests stale
download_coming_macos + download_coming_windows keys present in en.json but missing from TRANSLATIONS dict in i18n_site.py — 4 i18n tests failing
site.css.sha256 hash sidecar stale (CSS modified but hash file not regenerated) — 1 test failing
Homepage hero: 'The World's First Software 5.0 Platform' positioning vs target 'Linux of AI' — messaging gap
Compare page: missing n8n as competitor, missing P36 Tree of Knowledge row, missing P37 personas row, missing free-tier/BYOK rows, missing zero-telemetry row — coverage gap
Free tier messaging did not clearly state: unlimited local tasks, all 18 apps, BYOK zero markup, zero telemetry
fixes_applied: Updated test_checkout_flow.py: billing-success asserts bs-plan-badge/bs-features presence instead of old IDs
Updated test_checkout_flow.py: billing-cancel asserts 'charged' + 'free' presence instead of old headline text
Updated test_esign.py: TestPricingTiers now expects 5 tiers [Free, Starter, Pro, Team, Enterprise] with correct prices
Added download_coming_macos + download_coming_windows to TRANSLATIONS['en'] in i18n_site.py
Regenerated web/css/site.css.sha256 from actual file hash
Homepage hero: updated to 'The Linux of AI: Your AI. Your Data. Your Rules.' with free tier emphasis
Homepage pricing: updated free tier card to emphasize unlimited local tasks, all 18 apps, BYOK zero markup, zero telemetry

Compare page: added Linux of AI frame section, expanded table with n8n (8th competitor column), added P36 Tree of Knowledge row, P37 47 personas row (bolded), zero telemetry row, free tier unlimited row, BYOK row, FDA Part 11 bolded row, persistent AI allies row
Compare page: upgraded Why Governance Matters section with 6 value cards including P36/P37, free tier framing, and 'The Race That Matters'
Compare page: upgraded final CTA to 'Join the open alternative. Free forever.'

uplifts_applied: P17 (Stakes — the race for AI future)
P36 (Tree of Knowledge row in compare)
P37 (47 Expert Personas bolded in compare)
